# wikidata-federation

> A Claude Code skill (MIT, v0.1.0 Unreleased) that registers researchers, papers, and DOI-registered repositories as Wikidata items and cross-links them so that ORCID ↔ Wikidata QID ↔ DOI ↔ `graph.jsonld` form one mutually-referencing machine-readable node. The identifier-federation step of the [`authorship-strategy`](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy) research line, run after a DOI is minted. Safety-first: every write is shown to a human before sending, deduplication runs before any creation, credentials are never hardcoded.

## Core documentation

- [SKILL.md](skills/wikidata-federation/SKILL.md): full skill specification — safety discipline, prerequisites, six-phase workflow, property/QID conventions, pitfalls table
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing narrative (why federate, when to use, install, how it works, safety discipline, related skills)
- [CHANGELOG.md](CHANGELOG.md): release notes

## Why federate identifiers

ORCID, Zenodo, and GitHub are separate islands. A Wikidata item bridges them: once it exists, Scholia auto-generates an author profile, SPARQL can query every work, the CC0 dump propagates the node into downstream databases and LLM training corpora, and citation bots grow the graph without further author effort. Minting a DOI puts a record in one archive; the Wikidata item is what lets the rest of the open-data ecosystem find and cite it.

## Workflow phases

0. **Deduplication (mandatory, first)** — search by ORCID (`P496`), DOI (`P356`), and label before creating anything; a duplicate is a quality incident and deletion needs admin rights. Match found → switch from create to add-missing-statements.
1. **Metadata resolution (Zenodo side)** — resolve concept DOI vs version DOI with the human, pull the first-version publication date (the concept record reports the latest version's date), map `resource_type` to the correct `P31`.
2. **Dry run** — validate auth / CSRF / request shape against sandbox item `Q4115189` (or `test.wikidata.org`) via an alias add → remove round-trip.
3. **Item creation** — `gen_payload.py` turns a spec into a `wbeditentity` payload enforcing author/paper/software conventions; the payload is shown to the human; only after approval does `wd_api.py` send it. One item per call; read back from `Special:EntityData/<QID>.json`.
4. **`graph.jsonld` anchoring** — `add_qid_sameas.py` injects each QID into the `sameAs` of the matching node (exact identity match; Person matched on ORCID only) by format-preserving text surgery validated against an independently computed expected tree.
5. **Verification and reporting** — emit QID + URL list, the Scholia author URL, confirm `haswbstatement` index propagation, list residual manual tasks.

## Safety discipline

This skill writes to a shared public database, so it leads with constraints: no silent writes (full payload shown before every send), mandatory Phase 0 deduplication, no guessed retries (raw API response surfaced on error, then stop), credentials only via env var or `chmod 600` file, no Wikidata items for the author's own coined concepts (self-promotion / definitional-authority leak — concepts stay in the repo's `graph.jsonld`), no whole-file `graph.jsonld` re-serialization.

## Doctrine repository

- [authorship-strategy](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy): the normative framework whose identifier-federation discipline this skill operationalizes at the Wikidata layer

## Peer components

Other component skills of the same framework:

- [release-doi](https://github.com/shimo4228/release-doi): the release runbook this skill continues — it mints the DOI, this skill federates it
- [jsonld-knowledge-graph](https://github.com/shimo4228/jsonld-knowledge-graph): designs the `graph.jsonld` this skill's Phase 4 anchors QIDs into
- [llms-txt-writer](https://github.com/shimo4228/llms-txt-writer): the AI-facing-documentation peer in the same Layer 4 tactic

Terminology: *sibling* is reserved for research-line-level peers; at the component-skill level the term *peer component* is used.

## Sibling research lines

The skill federates artifacts in the author's research program:

- [authorship-strategy](https://github.com/shimo4228/authorship-strategy)
- [agent-knowledge-cycle (AKC)](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-knowledge-cycle) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19200726
- [contemplative-agent](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19212118
- [agent-attribution-practice (AAP)](https://github.com/shimo4228/agent-attribution-practice) — DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19652013
